description: Social Media interview questions for junior or entry-level candidates.

questions.question: What does engagement usually mean in social media?

choices.text: User actions such as likes, comments, shares, saves, clicks, or replies.
Only the number of employees on a marketing team.
The cost of office furniture.
A private password list.

questions.correctExplanation: Engagement measures how users interact with social media content.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Team size is unrelated to social engagement.
Office costs are not engagement metrics.

questions.question: Why is brand voice important on social media?

choices.text: It helps the brand communicate consistently and be recognizable across posts and platforms.
It means every post must use the exact same sentence.
It removes the need for audience research.
It only matters for printed advertisements.

questions.correctExplanation: A consistent brand voice helps build recognition, trust, and a clear personality.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Consistency does not mean repeating the same sentence.
Brand voice matters strongly in digital and social channels.

questions.question: A brand receives a negative comment on a public post. What should you usually do first?

choices.text: Review the comment, stay professional, respond appropriately, and move sensitive details to a private channel if needed.
Reply angrily to defend the brand.
Delete every negative comment immediately.
Ignore all comments forever.

questions.correctExplanation: A professional response protects the brand while showing that the concern is taken seriously.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Angry replies can damage brand reputation.
Deleting comments may be appropriate only if they violate policy, not as a default response.
Ignoring all comments can make the brand seem unresponsive.

questions.question: Why is a content calendar useful for social media management?

choices.text: It helps plan posts, maintain consistency, coordinate campaigns, and avoid last-minute confusion.
It guarantees every post will go viral.
It removes the need to check performance.
It is only useful after a campaign ends.

questions.correctExplanation: A content calendar supports planning, consistency, collaboration, and campaign organization.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Planning helps quality but does not guarantee virality.
Performance still needs to be measured.
Content calendars are most useful before and during execution.

questions.question: A new social media trend is becoming popular. What should a brand consider before joining it?

choices.text: Whether the trend fits the brand voice, audience, timing, and campaign goals.
Join immediately without understanding the trend.
Use the trend even if it conflicts with the brand's values.
Avoid all trends permanently.

questions.correctExplanation: Trends can create engagement, but they should be evaluated for relevance, tone, and risk.

wrongExplanations.explanation: Using a trend without context can lead to mistakes.
A trend that conflicts with brand values can damage trust.
Some trends can be useful when they fit the brand.
